text.content:
Stress and Simple Dice

Roll a d10. On a stress die, face 0 on the opening roll is a potential botch (storyguide assigns botch dice). Face 1 explodes (×2, ×4, …). On a simple die, face 0 counts as 10.

Characteristics & Abilities

Add the relevant scores to your die roll. When an ability has a specialty, you may apply +1 if that specialty applies to the action.

Confidence

Spend 1 Confidence for +3 on a roll (ability dialog and spontaneous casting offer a checkbox). Saga guides may allow other spends.

Attack & Defense

Attack = Weapon Atk Bonus + Dex + Attack Skill. Defense = Parry Bonus + Parry Skill − Size. Dodge = Dodge + Qik − Enc − Size.

When a weapon lists an Ability (Brawl, Single Weapon, Great Weapon, Bow, Thrown Weapon, Crossbow), combat totals use that ability score from the character’s ability Items. Otherwise the weapon’s Attack Skill / Parry Skill fields are used.

Damage & Soak

Damage = Weapon Damage + Str + Attack Skill, compared against Soak. Soak = Stamina + equipped armor Protection.

Wounds & Fatigue

Track body levels and fatigue levels on the character sheet status strip (visible on every tab). Wound and fatigue penalties are applied automatically to stress rolls and shown in the chat breakdown.

Weapons & Armor Pack

The Weapons & Armor compendium merges AG0201 charts (pp. 58–59) with optional Lords of Men extras. Where both editions list the same weapon or armor outfit, AG0201 numbers win. Remaining LoM-only gear is prefixed LoM — and marked as ArM5-scale reference.

Edition Policy

This system uses Ars Magica 2nd Edition combat and casting math. Later-edition OGL content (spells, virtues/flaws) remains available as reference material.

Formulaic Spells

Casting Modifier = Technique + Form + Stamina. Roll die + modifier vs spell Level.

  • Total ≥ Level: spell succeeds, no Fatigue.
  • Total < Level: lose 1 Fatigue; spell still works unless more than 10 short.

Spontaneous Spells

Click an Arts grid cell. Casting total = Technique + Form + Intelligence.

  • Fatiguing: (die + total) ÷ 2 — always costs 1 Fatigue.
  • Careful: ÷ 5 — no Fatigue.

The divided result is the Level equivalent of the effect. Compare to formulaic spells of those arts near that Level. Penetration uses the undivided roll.

Creatures

The Bestiary (ArM5 Ch.13) Actor pack includes sample Magic, Faerie, Infernal, and Divine creatures plus a few human NPC templates. Drag beasts onto the canvas; adapt ArM5 Init/Atk/Def/Dam and Might framing to 2e combat as needed.

Rituals & Vis

Ritual spells and raw vis uses follow the Magic chapter; track vis and long-term Fatigue narratively for now.

Formulaic Spells Compendium

The Formulaic Spells (ArM5 Index) compendium contains spell names, arts, and levels from Ars Magica Fifth Edition, Appendix III: Spells Index, made available under the Ars Magica Open License via Project: Redcap.

Index entries do not include full range, duration, or target parameters — add those from your saga's rulebooks as needed. General (Gen) spells are marked accordingly.

Based on Ars Magica material ©1993–2024, licensed by Trident, Inc. d/b/a Atlas Games®, under CC-BY-SA 4.0. Ars Magica, Mythic Europe, and related marks are trademarks of Trident, Inc., used with permission.

Lords of Men extras (merged)

LoM Arms & Armor OGL entries that do not conflict with AG0201 live in the single Weapons & Armor pack, named with a LoM — prefix.

Duplicates of AG0201 Melee / Missile / Armor Chart items were omitted; use the unprefixed AG0201 entries for 2e Soak and combat totals.

Source: Project: Redcap — Lords of Men Supplement: Arms & Armor (CC BY-SA 4.0).

Based on Ars Magica material ©1993–2024, licensed by Trident, Inc. d/b/a Atlas Games®, under CC-BY-SA 4.0.

Virtues & Flaws Compendium

The Virtues & Flaws pack merges short AG0201/2e core entries with the ArM5 Chapter IV OGL catalog. Where both editions share a name (or Affinity with Art / Affinity With (Art)), the 2e description and points win.

Import virtues and flaws in the Character Wizard or drag them onto the Virtues tab. Related Arm5-only entries such as Poor (Characteristic) remain available when they do not clash with a 2e stub.

Arm5 source: Project: Redcap (CC BY-SA 4.0).

Based on Ars Magica material ©1993–2024, licensed by Trident, Inc. d/b/a Atlas Games®, under CC-BY-SA 4.0.

Licensed PDF (not shipped)

The Ars Magica 2e core book PDF is not included in this system release. If you own AG0201 (or another licensed PDF), the GM can attach it to the world:

  1. Place the PDF in your Foundry user data or world assets folder.
  2. Open the Journal directory → right-click → Attach Rules PDF….
  3. Choose the file. Foundry creates ArM2 Core Rules (PDF) with a PDF journal page.

Characters can drop that journal onto Rules Journal UUID (Advanced — Links), or use the header Rules button once linked. Settings also store the world PDF path and journal UUID.

This HTML Rules Reference remains the searchable quick sheet for dice, combat, and casting.
